title: Daily Sodium Calculator for Hypertension 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Daily Sodium Maximum Calculator for Hypertension
description: Calculate your daily sodium limit based on blood pressure. WHO <2000mg, AHA <1500mg. Discover hidden sodium sources and lower blood pressure naturally.
intro: **Sodium** is the mineral most linked to high blood pressure. The WHO recommends under **2000 mg/day** (equivalent to 5 g of salt) for general health, but the American Heart Association recommends just **1500 mg/day** for people with hypertension and African Americans. The average American consumes **12 g of salt/day**—more than double the recommendation. Here's the catch: 75% comes hidden in processed foods, not from your salt shaker. This calculator shows your daily sodium limit based on your blood pressure status + provides a list of high-sodium foods so you learn to read nutrition labels properly. Cutting just 1 g of salt daily lowers blood pressure by approximately **2 mmHg** (Cochrane meta-analysis 2020).
keyTakeaway: Maximum sodium: **WHO 2000 mg**, **AHA 1500 mg** for hypertension. 1 g salt = 400 mg sodium. 75% of dietary sodium is hidden in processed foods.

example.title: Example: Patient with Hypertension
example.result: Your limit: **1500 mg of sodium = 3.75 g of salt/day**. Avoid processed meats, snacks, and canned foods.

explanation:
## What is Sodium

An electrolytic mineral. 99% of salt (NaCl) contains sodium. 1 g of salt = 400 mg of sodium.

## Recommendations

| Source | Maximum mg/day |
|---|---|
| WHO (general population) | 2000 mg |
| AHA (ideal) | 1500 mg |
| AHA (hypertension) | 1500 mg |
| DASH diet (strict) | 1500 mg |
| Actual US consumption | 4800 mg ⚠️ |

## Hidden Sodium Sources

| Food | Sodium (mg/100g) |
|---|---|
| Deli meat | 1200 |
| Hard cheese | 800 |
| Bread | 400 |
| Bouillon cube | 8000 |
| Soy sauce | 5500 |
| Pizza | 600 |
| Potato chips | 500 |
| Breakfast cereal | 500 |

## Low-Sodium Foods

- Fresh fruits and vegetables: <50 mg.
- Unprocessed meat: 70 mg.
- Cooked dried legumes: 5 mg.
- Egg: 140 mg.
- Natural dairy: 50–120 mg.

## Effect on Blood Pressure

- **-1 g salt daily = -2 mmHg systolic** (Cochrane).
- Reduction of 3 g (from 12 to 9) = -5 mmHg.

## Common Mistakes

1. Remove the salt shaker but continue eating processed foods.
2. Assume 'healthy' cheese and bread are low in sodium (they're not).
3. Use 'light' salt with potassium without checking with your doctor (caution with kidney disease or ACE inhibitors).

## Related Calculators

- [DASH Macros](/macros-dash-hypertension).
- [Daily Potassium Needed](/potassium-daily-needed).
- [Daily Magnesium Required](/magnesium-daily-required).

Consult your cardiologist for personalized advice.

faq.q: How much sodium should I eat per day?
faq.a: The WHO recommends less than **2000 mg daily**. The American Heart Association recommends **1500 mg** for people with hypertension.

faq.q: Can reducing salt lower blood pressure?
faq.a: Yes. Cutting 1 g of salt daily can lower systolic blood pressure by approximately **2 mmHg** (Cochrane meta-analysis).

faq.q: How much sodium is in 1 gram of salt?
faq.a: 1 gram of salt contains approximately **400 mg of sodium**.

faq.q: Is pink salt lower in sodium than regular salt?
faq.a: No. Pink Himalayan salt and table salt have essentially the same sodium content—mostly marketing.
A low-sodium diet limits intake to 1500–2000 mg daily, focusing on fresh, unprocessed foods and avoiding packaged items.

faq.q: How do I read sodium on nutrition labels?
faq.a: Look for milligrams (mg) of sodium per serving. Less than 120 mg is low; 120–600 mg is moderate; over 600 mg is high.

faq.q: Is low-sodium salt with potassium safe?
faq.a: Be cautious. If you have kidney disease or take ACE inhibitors, consult your doctor before using potassium-based salt substitutes.

faq.q: Where does hidden sodium come from?
faq.a: About **75% of dietary sodium** comes from processed foods—deli meats, bread, canned soups, cheese, sauces—not your salt shaker.
Blood pressure of **140/90 mmHg or higher** is hypertension. Prehypertension is 120–139/80–89 mmHg.
